test.note: 749/750 tests pass. Single pre-existing failure: TestConvertFileMatchesReferenceStructureIgnoringImages (OEBPS/c109.xhtml text mismatch) — existed before M1 changes and is in M4/M5 territory (content rendering). M1 commit only added documentation comments.

lint.note: Project uses go vet as lint (no separate lint tool configured in services.yaml)

nonBlockingIssues.description: Duplicate 'final_actions → N/A' entry in function mapping comment (cosmetic)
RubyGroups/RubyContents naming is counter-intuitive: fragment type 'structure' ($608) → RubyContents, 'ruby_content' ($756) → RubyGroups
Python's check_consistency() and check_fragment_usage() are intentionally not ported (validation-only, don't affect EPUB output). Should be documented as excluded.

appliedUpdates.description: Added singleton collapse design difference to python-porting-guide.md: Python's organize_fragments_by_type collapses single-entry categories into scalars; Go's typed fragmentCatalog struct makes this implicit.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note about Go using magic-byte detection (CONT/DRMION signatures) vs Python's extension-based dispatch (.kfx, .azw8, .ion, .kpf). This is a deliberate architectural decision for the on-device use case.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Python yj_book.py L248-280 checks datafile.ext; Go kfx_container.go L114-145 checks bytes.HasPrefix for magic bytes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Consider adding a fragment type SID reference table to speed up future parity audits. Workers currently have to manually verify SID→name mappings via catalog.ion lookups.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: M1 worker transcript shows multiple catalog.ion lookups for fragment type SIDs ($270=container, $262=font, $387=preview_images, $608=structure, $756=ruby_content).

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill effectiveness: parity-auditor skill worked well for this M1 documentation-only task.
rejectedObservations.reason: Not actionable as a library or guidance update. This is skill feedback, not codebase knowledge.
